Replace.Text: The Sikh Empire joins the world stage. Let the Panjab's rivers sustain your cities and the Khalsa carry your resolve across the ages.

Maharaja Ranjit Singh, Lion of the Panjab, your people look to you for strength in war and resolve in spirit. Let the five rivers nourish your cities and your fields, while the Khalsa stands ready to defend the faithful and strike down all who threaten your realm. Rule with courage, balance the temporal sword with the light of faith, and your empire shall be remembered in glory.

Cities founded on or adjacent to a river receive +2 [ICON_Food] Food and +1 [ICON_Faith] Faith in the City Center. Farms adjacent to a river receive +1 [ICON_Faith] Faith. Farms adjacent to both a river and a Holy Site receive +1 [ICON_Production] Production. After researching Irrigation, river-adjacent Farms receive an additional +1 [ICON_Food] Food. After researching Civil Engineering, river-adjacent Farms receive +1 [ICON_Production] Production. Floodplains provide +1 [ICON_Faith] Faith. All combat units receive +5 [ICON_Strength] Combat Strength against units of a civilization following a rival majority religion.

All land combat units ignore combat penalties from being wounded. Defeating enemy units grants [ICON_Faith] Faith equal to 50% of the defeated unit's Combat Strength. Cities receive +15% [ICON_Production] Production toward military units.

Sikh unique Medieval melee unit that replaces the Man-at-Arms. Stronger, faster, can be purchased with [ICON_Faith] Faith, ignores combat penalties from being wounded, and gains +5 [ICON_Strength] Combat Strength.
Ignores combat penalties from being wounded and gains +5 [ICON_Strength] Combat Strength.

Sikh unique Industrial heavy cavalry unit that replaces the Cuirassier. Much stronger and faster, can be purchased with [ICON_Faith] Faith, ignores combat penalties from being wounded, gains +4 [ICON_Strength] Combat Strength, and pillages for only 1 Movement.
Ignores combat penalties from being wounded, gains +4 [ICON_Strength] Combat Strength, and pillages for only 1 Movement.
Sikh unique building that replaces the Temple. Provides +4 [ICON_Faith] Faith, +2 [ICON_Food] Food, +1 Housing, +1 Great Prophet point per turn, and +1 Great General point per turn.
Ignores combat penalties from being wounded.
+5 [ICON_Strength] Combat Strength against units of a civilization following a rival majority religion.

Ranjit Singh leads a faith-backed military civilization that thrives along rivers. Secure floodplains, settle aggressively on river systems, and convert faith from farms, Gurdwaras, and battlefield victories into pressure.

The Sikh Empire emerged in the late eighteenth and early nineteenth centuries from the Sikh misls of the Panjab. Under Maharaja Ranjit Singh, Lahore became the center of a powerful state known for cavalry, disciplined armies, religious patronage, and control of the river-fed heartland.
